dashboards.description: Per-vCenter breakdown of VM vCPU by role (VKS Worker, Supervisor CP, vCLS, VM Service, Regular) plus physical cores, vCPU:pCore ratio, and the computed VKS core equivalent. One row per vCenter — no scoping widget, this is a read-at-a-glance report. Replaces the per-vCenter PowerCLI VKS core- consumption script.
dashboards.name: [AI Content] VKS Core Consumption

dashboards.description: Pick a datastore-type bucket (vSAN, NFS, Local VMFS, or Shared VMFS) from the resource list on the left. The VM Performance + Storage view on the right narrows to that custom group and surfaces worst-offender VMs across CPU, memory, and virtualDisk IOPS/latency dimensions.

dashboards.description: Highlight ESXi Host configurations that need your attention. Use this dashboard to fix incorrect settings, update outdated components and reduce overall cost by optimizing your environment. The dashboard is organized top down, where configuration that needs your attention is shown first. No data on a chart means the configuration is optimized.
dashboards.name: vCommunity/ESXi Configuration 2.0

dashboards.description: Monitor the capacity of VMs. This dashboard lists all the hosts with their capacity remaining and time remaining. Select a VM and its detailed capacity over time will be automatically shown.

dashboards.name: vCommunity/VM Capacity 2.0

dashboards.description:
This is the main dashboards to monitor and troubleshoot vSphere VM Performance. This focuses on the contention metrics as they are the primary counter for performance, while utilization is the secondary counter. 

Operationalize Your World enhancement: 
added link to Guest OS dashboard.

dashboards.description: Monitor the capacity of vSphere clusters. It lists all the clusters with their capacity remaining, time remaining and VM remaining. Select a cluster and its detail capacity over time will be automatically shown. If you have an unbalanced cluster, use the ESXi capacity dashboard to see the details.

widgets.title: Running VMs in the selected Cluster or ESXi

config.title: Running VMs in the selected Cluster or ESXi

config.editorData:
Use this table to check if the cluster or host performance problem were caused by VM configuration and usage.

Note that it is possible that the VM was not on the same host at the time of problem, due to vMotion.

To drill down into a VM, select it and click the double arrow before the widget title.
config.title: Read Me

config.editorData:
Note that if a datastore is shared by >1 cluster, the counter at the datastore could be influenced by other clusters.

To drill down into a datastore, select it and click on the double arrow before the widget title.
config.title: Read Me
